message:
feat(btree): learned-rowid index with split-conformal radius (IMPL-22 / AAC-P8)

Adds `crates/fsqlite-btree/src/learned_rowid.rs` — a standalone rowid->page
learned-index module with formal coverage guarantees. Ships the model + tests
only; NOT wired into any seek path on this commit.

## Math

Linear regression via closed-form ordinary least squares:

    slope     = Cov(x, y) / Var(x)
    intercept = mean(y) - slope * mean(x)

Zero-variance x collapses safely to `(0, mean(y))`.

Split-conformal calibration (Vovk, "Algorithmic Learning in a Random World",
2005):

1. Deterministic 70/30 stride split of `(rowid, page)` observations into a
   fit set and a calibration set.
2. Fit slope/intercept on the fit set.
3. On the calibration set of size n, compute residuals
   r_i = |predicted_i - actual_i|, sort ascending.
4. Conformal radius = r_k where k = ceil((1 - alpha) * (n + 1)), 1-indexed
   and clamped to [1, n]. The `(n + 1)` term is the finite-sample correction
   for split conformal prediction.

Under exchangeability of calibration and test points, the marginal coverage
guarantee is `P(|predicted - actual| <= r_k) >= 1 - alpha`.

## What's exposed

- `struct LearnedRowIdIndex { slope, intercept, residuals_calibration, alpha }`
- `fn fit(rowid_to_page: &[(i64, u32)], alpha: f64) -> Self` — fits + calibrates
- `fn predict(&self, rowid: i64) -> (u32, u32)` — returns `(center_page, radius)`
- `fn page_range(&self, rowid: i64) -> RangeInclusive<u32>` — saturating-range
  wrapper around predict
- `fn radius(&self) -> u32` — cached conformal radius (u32::MAX sentinel for
  degenerate fits: empty input, single data point, no calibration)

Degenerate inputs (empty, singleton, all-equal rowid) are handled without
panicking. Very negative / very positive rowids saturate to `u32` bounds
via `saturating_sub`/`saturating_add`. `alpha` is clamped to `[0, 1]`.

## Tests

9 tests pass (unit + property):

- `fit_uniform_dense_is_low_rmse`: fit to 10k rowids with 100 rows/page
  gives slope ~ 0.010, intercept ~ -0.495, RMSE = 0.070 pages (essentially
  perfect linear fit).
- `conformal_coverage_meets_guarantee`: on a held-out test set with
  alpha=0.1, empirical coverage is 100% (>= 1 - alpha - 0.03 slack).
- `prop_conformal_coverage_uniform_dense`: proptest over 256 seeded
  train/test splits with alpha in [0.05, 0.3]; every case meets the
  coverage bound within finite-sample slack (0.04).
- Edge cases: empty input, single data point, all-equal rowids,
  `page_range` saturation at `u32::MAX` / `0`, `alpha` out-of-range
  clamping, `predict` center always contained in `page_range`.

## Wiring (pending; intentionally deferred)

When integrating into B-tree seek paths later:

- B-tree rowid seek can call `LearnedRowIdIndex::page_range(rowid)`, probe
  the pager cache in that range first; on cache miss, fall through to
  canonical `move_to_rowid` descent. Cost: O(radius) cache probes on the
  happy path vs. O(log n) disk descents on the cold path.
- Build the index on `ANALYZE`, from a sample of `(rowid, page)` pairs
  collected during a full scan (or incrementally during inserts).
- Invalidate on any balance / split / merge that shifts the rowid-to-page
  map. A generation counter on the per-table LearnedRowIdIndex is
  sufficient; stale readers see a mismatch and fall through.
- For skewed or multi-modal rowid distributions, upgrade to a piecewise
  linear model (already prototyped in `learned_index.rs` for key->position
  lookup); the conformal wrapper is independent of the underlying
  predictor.

## Constraints honored

- No unsafe (workspace forbid).
- No dependency on the rest of the btree crate.
- `connection.rs` untouched.
- Clippy pedantic + nursery clean; `cargo fmt` clean.

Refs: Vovk et al. "Algorithmic Learning in a Random World" (2005); Kraska
et al. "The Case for Learned Index Structures", SIGMOD 2018.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
